Hey everybody, welcome back to the module on beliefs. Now you might have heard me say many times in the past that if there's one skill in my life that's allowed me to make a fortune and help lots of people along the way, it's the power to influence and persuade. See this is the single skill out of everything I have in my arsenal that allows me to make money and achieve what I want in my life and also give back in meaningful ways. Now conversely if there's one element that's caused me the most pain in my life and that has caused me almost to sabotage my own success at times, it was my beliefs that I had. Essentially beliefs about myself, about wealth and success, about money, the way that I managed fear in the process as well. See your beliefs and most importantly the beliefs that you have about yourself are the single greatest factor that can hold you back from achieving success. And to get more specific it's the actual beliefs that you have about whether or not you're going to succeed, meaning before you take action, whether you believe that you're going to actually achieve success, those are the ones that truly affect you the most. For example you can be in a situation where you have the best tools, the best resources, the best training and even the opportunity itself is amazing yet you find yourself not taking action because you simply don't believe that you're going to succeed. And what happens is you're governed by essentially this lack of belief and you end up almost cubby-holing yourself into a far lower level of success than you're truly capable of achieving. You see the beliefs we have about what we're capable of define our actions more than anything else. If you think I'm a great closer or I'm an outstanding businessman or I'm unlucky and love even, those beliefs will actually determine what you'll do in life and what you won't do and what you'll try and what you'll not try. Now what happens is your brain actually starts to project forward what it thinks might happen in the future and this is called future-pacing your actions. It thinks about what might happen when you take action and decides whether or not there's actually a good likelihood of success. If your brain decides there's not a good likelihood of success your brain says don't do it. Your motivation strategy says nope, not going to work, you're not worth all that effort, too much risk there and you simply don't take action. This is why we don't try things that we don't think we're good at or that we don't think we'll truly succeed at. Now the flip side to this coin is to actually have empowering beliefs about what you're capable of that will propel you to take action. See for most of us we're actually capable of much more than we ever can really imagine. Now let me give you an example here. Imagine that you were truly confident that you would close pretty much anyone who was closeable and you had this belief you could handle any situation in business that was thrown at you. Now you're dropping through a totally strange alien environment where you don't know any by you don't have any opportunities that are actually obvious right now. I guarantee that somehow you'll still figure out a way to make money because you have that core belief that no matter where I go what I do I will figure out a way. In fact my best friend's father, this is my friend Alan Lipsky, when we were growing up his dad used to say you know if you flush Jordan down a toilet bowl he'll end up coming back up with a plumber's license. What that is that's the power of what's called self-belief. In other words you put me in a bad situation you know I'll make lemonade out of lemons and it starts from this idea that I have not myself that you know within my arsenal of tools I pretty much have everything that someone would need to achieve success in any situation and of course it starts with the ability to close anyone who's closeable. You see I know that I can close virtually anyone who's closeable and that I've taught thousands of others to do the same and to me that really represents the core of a belief that I have that I can succeed in any situation no matter what and that truly is what propels me to essentially strive for success no matter what obstacles are standing in my way. So I want to include this module in the training because yes of course you're going to dramatically increase your sales with a straight line system but I also want to make sure that you're not sabotaging your own success and that you're actually maximizing your potential for everything just besides business to really to be a better human being and to feel better about yourself each day when you're taking action to have less fear in your life. I want just to make sure that you felt really really good in the entire process. Now the other important piece here is that your prospects also have their own sets of beliefs that sometimes are empowering and sometimes very disempowering when it comes to whether or not they'll actually end up taking action. See if you know what's getting in the way for your clients in terms of their beliefs you can actually help them move past them so there are no roadblocks to them investing in your idea your product of your service.
